zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Joan De Loudham於1301年出生在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾史道頓時，她的父親Lord Robert I de Kirkton de Loudham26歲，母親Cecily DeLoudham21歲。她於1325年與Sir Geoffrey de Staunton結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1365年7月31日在她的家鄉享年64歲，埋葬在英國諾廷加姆爾史道頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Joan De Loudham于1301年出生在英国英格兰諾廷加姆爾史道頓时，她的父亲Lord Robert I de Kirkton de Loudham26岁，母亲Cecily DeLoudham21岁。她于1325年与Sir Geoffrey de Staunton结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1365年7月31日在她的家乡享年64岁，埋葬在英国諾廷加姆爾史道頓。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Loudham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: ‘tendril’ or ‘creeper’, often used in metaphors describing the slender curvature of eyebrows, arms, hair, swords, lightning, and women's bodies.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Joan De Loudham was born in 1301, in Staunton, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Lord Robert I de Kirkton de Loudham, was 26 and her mother, Cecily DeLoudham, was 21. She married Sir Geoffrey de Staunton in 1325. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 31 July 1365, in her hometown, at the age of 64, and was buried in Staunton, Nottinghamshire, England.

Elle a épousé Sir Geoffrey de Staunton en 1325. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 31 juillet 1365, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 64 ans, et a été enterrée à Staunton, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre.

ko.Staunton, Newark, Nottinghamshire, Engla: 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 스타운턴 인 테 발
ko.lifeSummary: Lady Joan De Loudham(이)가 1301년 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 스타운턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Lord Robert I de Kirkton de Loudham(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Cecily DeLoudham(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1325년 Sir Geoffrey de Staunton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1365년 7월 31일 그녀의 고향에서 64세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노틴그함실르 스타운턴에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Joan De Loudham родилась в 1301 г. в местности стаунтон, ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Lord Robert I de Kirkton de Loudham было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Cecily DeLoudham, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Geoffrey de Staunton в 1325 г. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла 31 июля 1365 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 64 лет и была похоронена в местности стаунтон, ноттингхамшир, Англия.
